name: N'Ko (Manding koinรฉ)
notes: N'Ko (฿฿฿), the Manding literary koinรฉ written in the N'Ko script devised by Solomana Kantรฉ in 1949. INPUT CONTRACT: native N'Ko-script text (Unicode block U+07C0..U+07FF, NFC), written RIGHT-TO-LEFT; no romanisation is accepted. N'Ko is an ALPHABET (not an abjad or abugida): seven vowel letters (฿ ฿ ฿ ฿ ฿ ฿ ฿) and nineteen consonant letters are written in full, plus combining diacritics. Tone and vowel length are written with seven combining marks (U+07EB..U+07F1): they are mapped to IPA tone letters (and ห for the long series) rather than to segments; an unmarked vowel is short with a falling tone. U+07F2 (nasalisation) maps to a combining tilde. ฿ DAGBASINNA is a silent letter marking the absence of a vowel in a consonant cluster and maps to the empty string. LEFT OUT ON PURPOSE: U+07F3 (double dot above) and the JONA letters ฿จ ฿ฉ ฿ช, which modify a base letter for non-native sounds in loans โ their value depends on the base letter and no single segmental value can be assigned.
